question_text: A coal power station burns 1000 kg of coal per hour. If coal releases 30 MJ of energy per kilogram, calculate the energy released in one hour.
explanation: Energy = Mass × Energy per unit mass. Energy = 1000 kg × 30 MJ/kg = 30,000 MJ. This can also be written as 30 GJ (gigajoules) or 3 × 10^10 joules.
